user_story: Improve MR widget warning when pipeline is skipped and merging is allowed - ### 🧩 Problem Statement When the merge checks for a project allow merging with skipped pipelines, it is not clearly communicated to users through the MR widget message and button variant used. | Image | |-------| | ![Screen_Shot_2021-05-13_at_12.50.43_PM](/uploads/77e60fa1d6454e73e5552ae38e6b07a6/Screen_Shot_2021-05-13_at_12.50.43_PM.png) | ### 🧾 Background https://gitlab.com/groups/gitlab-org/-/epics/5998 ### Proposal - Re-evaluate the success variant - Add a modal with a message to communicate to users that the pipeline was skipped and merging could have consequences --------- Reproduced here: https://gitlab.com/ci-sample-scenarios/must-succeed-skipped-allowed
defect_reasoning: La historia presenta un problema claro y un valor para el usuario, pero carece de claridad en la descripción de cómo se medirá el éxito y no está completamente estructurada en términos de requisitos específicos. Además, la propuesta de solución es un poco ambigua y podría beneficiarse de una mayor especificidad.

user_story: Redirect back to "new release" page after creating milestone - ### Summary When creating a new release, if you create a new milestone within the workflow - the milestone is created, but the release is not. ### Steps to reproduce <!-- Describe how one can reproduce the issue - this is very important. Please use an ordered list. --> 1. Go to project overview->releases 1. Create a new release 1. Fill in all the details until you reach milestone 1. Create a **new** milestone 1. Fill in all the details for the milestone and complete the creation 1. Go back to releases ### What is the current *bug* behavior? Creating a new milestone kicks you out of the create release flow ### What is the expected *correct* behavior? A milestone is created within the release creation (similar to creating a new environment in the feature flag workflow) or at least after milestone creation, return to the create release form with all the previous details filled out I created a video that displays the workflow: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tZIPG0eRTwU ### Proposal Update the New Milestone page to allow the behavior of the Create Milestone button to be configurable: - By default, clicking it would navigate to the new milestone's detail page (as it does today). - However, if a `back_url` URL parameter is provided when navigating the **Create Milestone** page, the user will be redirected to the provided URL instead. Also implement https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/372716
defect_reasoning: El PBI describe un comportamiento anómalo con pasos claros para reproducir el error, así como el comportamiento esperado. Sin embargo, la propuesta de solución es un poco ambigua y no se detalla completamente cómo se implementará.

user_story:
Setup link checker for docs in CI - As a **manitainer**
I want to **automatically check if all used URLs in docs are still valid when committing**
so that **we prevent dead links and solve them as quickly as possible when they happen anyway** **:white\_check\_mark: Acceptance criteria** 1. docs.nlx.io and appstore.commonground.nl/docs 1. build fails when internal links are dead 1. daily job running for link checking (only give warning in ci/cd) **:hammer\_and\_wrench: Proposed technical solution** * In the CI: use [muffet](https://github.com/raviqqe/muffet) in a container linked to the to-be-released docs container to verify all URL's to be valid. * **:link: Reference links** *
defect_reasoning: El PBI describe una tarea técnica clara, pero carece de un contexto sistémico definido y no menciona el impacto medible de la implementación. La mitigación de riesgos no está especificada, lo que afecta la puntuación.
